skills.name: debug-issue
skills.description: Debug an issue systematically by identifying reproduction steps, tracing likely code paths, forming grounded hypotheses, and proposing or implementing the smallest safe fix. Use when investigating a bug, failure, or unexpected behavior.

skills.name: implement-from-ticket
skills.description: Implement a feature or fix from ticket text by extracting requirements, inferring local conventions from the repository, keeping scope tight, and delivering the smallest complete solution. Use when working from Jira, Linear, GitHub issue, or similar ticket content.

skills.name: reduce-complexity
skills.description: Simplify overly complex code while preserving behavior. Use when a function, component, module, or flow is harder to read, reason about, test, or maintain than necessary.

skills.name: review-changed-files
skills.description: Review only files modified in the current branch for bugs, regressions, edge cases, maintainability issues, and missing validation. Use when assessing the quality and risk of branch changes without drifting into unrelated code.

skills.name: write-tests
skills.description: Write or update tests only for files modified in the current branch. Use the repository’s existing testing library, helpers, conventions, and file patterns. Do not add or modify tests for untouched files unless a directly related shared test helper must be adjusted.

skills.name: create-acceptance-criteria
skills.description: Create clear, testable acceptance criteria from a feature, user story, bug fix, or product request. Use when requirements need to be specific enough for engineering, QA, and review.

skills.name: write-user-stories
skills.description: Turn a feature idea, product request, or rough problem statement into clear user stories with scope, context, and acceptance direction. Use when product work needs to be structured for implementation.

skills.name: edge-case-hunter
skills.description: Identify edge cases, boundary conditions, and failure modes for a feature, bug fix, workflow, or API. Use when trying to reduce surprises before implementation, review, or release.

skills.name: test-plan
skills.description: Create a practical test plan for a feature, bug fix, or release by identifying critical scenarios, risks, edge cases, and validation areas. Use when planning QA coverage before or during implementation.

skills.name: branch-review
skills.description: Review the current branch holistically by summarizing its intent, changed areas, risk level, test coverage quality, and likely follow-up concerns. Use when preparing a branch for merge or explaining its overall quality.

skills.name: issue-writer
skills.description: Turn a rough problem statement, bug, request, or idea into a clear issue with context, scope, expected outcome, and implementation guidance where appropriate. Use for GitHub issues, Linear tasks, Jira tickets, or internal tracking.

skills.name: pr-description
skills.description: Write a clear pull request description from branch changes, commit history, ticket text, or notes. Use when preparing a PR for reviewers and you want a concise, useful summary with context, scope, risks, and test notes.

skills.name: commit
skills.description: Review current git changes, validate them, and create a clean commit

skills.name: pr
skills.description: Create a pull request from the current branch with a strong title and description

skills.name: write-status-update
skills.description: Turn raw notes, tickets, commits, or progress bullets into a clear status update with progress, current state, risks, and next steps. Use for weekly updates, project updates, and stakeholder communication.
